name: laser-session
description: The canonical SFX workflow: author a laser zap, surgically retune it, spin three level-matched round-robin variants, and organise everything into an engine-ready bank. Replay with: sonarium replay docs/examples/laser-session.json

steps.note: Author the base sound: a descending band-limited square (880→180 Hz exponential slide, the 'pew') layered with a 40 ms noise transient for the trigger snap. Everything else in this session derives from this one graph.
Surgical edit — widen the pulse from 25% to 50% duty for a rounder tone. set_param re-renders without re-sending the graph; the path addresses the square node inside the first mul branch.
Three round-robin takes, each perturbed by 15% and loudness-matched to −16 LUFS. The fixed seed (42) makes the variants reproducible — replaying this file regenerates byte-identical takes.
A bank is the engine-facing pack: stable id ('blaster_pack'), exported later with a sounds.json manifest.
Tag the original as a 'blaster' round-robin member in the 'weapon' category — engines cycle rr_group members so repeats never sound identical.
The first variant joins the same round-robin group.
